rubric.criterion: Compares ch against each of "a", "e", "i", "o", "u" using .equals (not ==), returning true on any match and false otherwise.
Checks phrase == null first (or short-circuits) and then checks for length 0, returning 0 in those cases without throwing NullPointerException.

rubric.point_label: Word splitting via indexOf and substring (part b)
rubric.criterion: Uses indexOf(" ") together with substring to extract each word, advancing past the space; does not use String.charAt.
Extracts the first character of each word using substring(0, 1) — not charAt.
countVowelWords delegates the vowel test to isVowel rather than reimplementing the comparison.
Maintains an int counter incremented exactly when isVowel returns true for a word's first letter, and returns that counter.

test_cases.description: basic phrase with mixed words
test_cases.setup_java:
PhraseScanner ps = new PhraseScanner("apple orange banana igloo kite");
System.out.println(ps.countVowelWords());

test_cases.description: null and empty inputs
single word and isVowel direct checks

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the method throwDart, which returns a pseudo-random integer in the range from 1 to maxScore inclusive. Use Math.random() to generate the random value.

parts.label: (b)
parts.prompt: Write the method bullseyePercent, which simulates numThrows dart throws by calling throwDart for each throw. A throw is a bullseye when the value returned by throwDart equals maxScore. The method must return the integer percentage (0 to 100) of throws that were bullseyes. You must use throwDart from part (a) to receive full credit. Assume numThrows is greater than 0.

rubric.criterion: Uses Math.random() to produce a random value inside throwDart.

rubric.point_label: Correct range scaling and int cast (part a)
rubric.criterion: Multiplies Math.random() by maxScore, adds 1, and casts to int so result spans [1, maxScore] inclusive.
Uses a loop with bounds for (int i = 0; i < numThrows; i++) or equivalent that runs exactly numThrows iterations.
Compares the returned dart value to maxScore and increments a counter only when they are equal.
Part (b) calls throwDart() from part (a) instead of reimplementing the random logic.
Casts hits or numThrows to double before dividing so the percentage computation is not truncated to 0.
Returns an int from bullseyePercent representing the integer percentage of bullseyes.

test_cases.description: guaranteed bullseye when maxScore is 1
test_cases.setup_java:
DartGame g = new DartGame(1, 50);
System.out.println(g.bullseyePercent());

test_cases.description: throwDart returns value in valid range
many throws on a single-score board still 100%
test_cases.setup_java:
DartGame g = new DartGame(1, 250);
System.out.println(g.bullseyePercent());

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the method containsBanned, which returns true if the parameter word contains the String bannedLetter at any position, and returns false otherwise. You must extract characters from word using substring; do not use charAt.

parts.label: (b)
parts.prompt: Write the method countFlagged, which returns the number of words in comment that contain bannedLetter. Words in comment are separated by single spaces. You must call containsBanned from part (a) to receive full credit. Use indexOf and substring to split comment into individual words.

rubric.point_label: Loop over characters of word (part a)
rubric.criterion: Loops over indices 0 to word.length() - 1 inclusive to inspect each character of word.

rubric.point_label: String character extraction via substring (part a)
rubric.criterion: Uses word.substring(i, i + 1) and .equals(bannedLetter) to test each character; does not use charAt.
Returns true on first match and false after the loop completes with no match.

rubric.point_label: Splits comment using indexOf and substring (part b)
rubric.criterion: Uses indexOf(" ") and substring to peel words from comment one at a time.

rubric.point_label: Handles final word after last space (part b)
rubric.criterion: Processes the trailing word that has no space after it (or otherwise visits every word including the last).
Part (b) calls containsBanned() rather than reimplementing the substring search inline.

rubric.point_label: Accumulator and return value correct (part b)
rubric.criterion: Initializes count to 0, increments only when containsBanned returns true, and returns the int count.

test_cases.description: single word with banned letter
test_cases.setup_java:
MessageFilter mf = new MessageFilter("z");
System.out.println(mf.containsBanned("pizza"));

test_cases.description: multi-word comment count
test_cases.setup_java:
MessageFilter mf = new MessageFilter("a");
System.out.println(mf.countFlagged("apple banana cherry kiwi"));

test_cases.description: no flagged words in comment
test_cases.setup_java:
MessageFilter mf = new MessageFilter("x");
System.out.println(mf.countFlagged("hello world from java"));
